search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG145 thyroid enlargement focal nodularity hoarse voice swallowing difficulty ultrasound
candidate_sources_rejected: thyroid-cancer guidance used to label every nodule malignant
automatic ultrasound, cytology, scoring, biopsy, surgery, or referral rules
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: A thyroid nodule may be incidental, palpable, benign, inflammatory, or malignant and the workflow cannot infer cause.
Investigation and management decisions remain clinician-entered and local-pathway dependent.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults, children, and young people with a clinician-recognised thyroid lump or focal nodularity; paediatric, pregnancy, postpartum, inflammatory, post-procedure, and known-cancer contexts require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary or specialist outpatient thyroid-nodule documentation.
UAE_applicability: NICE documentation domains transfer partially; UAE imaging, cytology, endocrine, surgery, oncology, and referral pathways govern.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG145 recommendations and 2025 surveillance status were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: NG145 remains current and was last updated in 2023.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact thyroid-enlargement section supports documenting onset or growth context, pressure-related symptoms, relevant negatives, focused thyroid or neck examination, and already available thyroid results without diagnosing a benign or malignant nodule.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of palpable thyroid enlargement or focal nodularity, recommendation-qualified ultrasound, compression symptoms, and clinician-led specialist assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact information and warning-symptom section supports patient concerns, clinician concern, safety-net documentation, an entered plan, and questions only when discussed; it does not create referral or emergency instructions.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports shared decision documentation, thyroid-medicine interaction information, and specific thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms including shortness of breath, rapid growth, hoarse voice, and swallowing difficulty.

unresolved_source_gaps: How the nodule was identified, exact growth, pain, tenderness, voice, swallowing, breathing, thyroid dysfunction, inflammatory symptoms, irradiation, family history, pregnancy, ultrasound, cytology, thyroid function, nodes, diagnosis, referral, surveillance, and plan remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
